bead.title: agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run
bead.description:
Stage 2 of SD-024 cleanup. Removes all three legacy options types and the legacy Run method on the agent runner. Renames RunWithConfig to Run.

Depends on bead 16 (Stage 1 cleanup) AND beads 17-21 (every Stage 2 caller migrated).

In-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — delete RunOptions, CompareOptions, QuorumOptions structs
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go — delete legacy Run, rename RunWithConfig
bead.acceptance: field RunOptions removed; field CompareOptions removed; field QuorumOptions removed. (Yes the gate currently parses 'field' rather than 'type'; intentional — re-uses the field_removed verifier whose go-source regex `\bX\s+[\*\[\]\w.]+` matches both `Name Type` field declarations AND `type Name struct` type declarations. The gate refuses already_satisfied if any of these symbols still appear at a declaration site in cli/internal/agent/.) cd cli && go build ./... passes. cd cli && go test ./... passes. Manual verification: `rg '^type (RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions) ' cli/internal/agent/types.go` returns zero.

events.body:
Bead ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

STATUS: not attempted; scope-vs-budget mismatch between bead description
and actual codebase state. Request re-scoping into sub-beads (see below)
before another execution attempt.

================================================================
What is done
================================================================

Stage 2 dispatch-site migrations (beads c0ff5d3e, 4c31e465, 21f9e321,
ee2cd434) landed RunWithConfig / RunCompareWithConfigViaService /
RunQuorumWithConfigViaService alongside the legacy Run /
RunCompareViaService / RunQuorumViaService entry points. The CLI
dispatch sites (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`, `ddx agent
quorum`) now construct rcfg + AgentRunRuntime / CompareRuntime /
QuorumRuntime and dispatch through the new `*WithConfig*` paths.
A subset of agent test files were migrated by bead ee2cd434.

Stage 1 cleanup (bead edbfa046) removed ExecuteBeadLoopOptions and
the legacy `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run(opts)` method, renamed
`RunWithConfig` → `Run`. That cleanup touched 18 files and ~123
net-changed lines.

================================================================
Why this bead cannot land in one pass as currently scoped
================================================================

The bead description in-scopes only two files:

  - cli/internal/agent/types.go (delete RunOptions / CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions structs)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go (delete legacy Run, rename
    RunWithConfig → Run)

The bead acceptance criteria, however, require `cd cli && go build
./...` and `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass. After the structs and
the legacy method are deleted, the tree does not compile. A grep
over the post-Stage-2 tree shows the deleted symbols are still
referenced in:

  RunOptions: 137 occurrences across 42 files
  CompareOptions: ~15 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    models.go, agent_compare_config_test.go, plus 4 testdata files
  QuorumOptions: ~5 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    agent_quorum_config_test.go, plus testdata

Stage 2's dispatch-site migrations only retired *construction* of
these structs at the four CLI entry points and a portion of
agent-package tests. The structs are still load-bearing for:

  - Internal helpers in cli/internal/agent: BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
    resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,
    runVirtualFn, RunAgent, runScriptFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn,
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and the
    entire compare_adapter.go internal pipeline (CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions are used as the working struct shape inside
    RunCompare / RunQuorum, not just at the top of the public API).
  - The `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` self-call inside
    runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity (line 976).
  - The `r.Run(RunOptions{...})` call inside grade.go::169.
  - The `agent.RunViaService` exported function and its internal
    callers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, cli/internal/server/server.go
    at lines 1913 and 3786, cli/internal/exec/store.go:201).
  - The `Run(opts agent.RunOptions)` interface contract on
    cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 plus its three implementers
    (cmd/exec.go, internal/exec/store.go, exec_acceptance_test.go,
    workers_stop_propagation_test.go).
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go's two test runners
    (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture).
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go's
    resolvePrompt / defaultResolvePromptForCompare /
    RunViaServiceWith calls (the bead 21 commit explicitly noted
    these were left out of scope).
  - cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/* — the testdata uses a *separate*
    stub `agent.RunOptions` in testdata/src/agent/agent.go that is
    independent of the production type, so testdata compiles fine,
    but analyzer.go's package-level doc comments and
    runOptionsTypeNames list reference the production type's name.

================================================================
Estimated true cost
================================================================

Stage 1's analogous cleanup (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) was 18 files /
~123 net lines. Stage 2's RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
cleanup is ~3-4× larger by raw reference count, and ~2× larger
structurally because (a) RunOptions is used as the central internal
data carrier inside compare_adapter.go's full Run / RunCompare /
RunQuorum pipelines (not just at the dispatch entry), and (b)
external callers in three different packages (cmd/, internal/exec/,
internal/server/) each need their own rcfg + AgentRunRuntime
construction wired in, which means the `agent.RunViaService` /
`agent.RunCompareViaService` / `agent.RunQuorumViaService`
back-compat shims must stay or be replaced.

Conservative estimate: ~40 files / ~1500–2500 net lines / 3-5×
the per-bead size budget defined in SD-024 §"Per-bead size budget"
(target <500 LoC, <20 minutes, <$10 spend).

A no-change here is preferable to a half-finished commit that
leaves the tree red or smuggles a partial migration past review.

================================================================
What a follow-up attempt would need
================================================================

Recommend splitting into a fresh stage-2-cleanup sub-stage with
beads sized to the SD-024 budget. Suggested decomposition:

  1. Migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go internals to
     drive RunCompare / RunQuorum through AgentRunRuntime + rcfg
     end-to-end; retire CompareOptions and QuorumOptions struct
     usage *inside* the agent package.

  2. Migrate cli/internal/agent helpers (BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
     resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,
     runVirtual, RunAgent, runScript, runClaudeWithFallback,
     SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare) so they
     take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime (or per-field args) instead of
     RunOptions. Includes runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity and
     grade.go's `r.Run` self-call.

  3. Migrate cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService et
     al.) to take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime and update all three
     callers: cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, server.go:1913+3786,
     internal/exec/store.go:201.

  4. Migrate cli/internal/exec (types.go interface +
     store.go construction + store_test.go mock) and
     cmd/exec.go's serviceExecAgentRunner +
     exec_acceptance_test.go mock onto the rcfg-based interface.

  5. Migrate the remaining agent-package test sites that bead
     ee2cd434 left out of scope (prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
     execute_bead_runtime_test.go, models_test.go,
     session_index_test.go, virtual_test.go, claude_stream_test.go,
     and the BuildArgs tests in agent_test.go that still construct
     RunOptions literals as inputs to the unit under test).

  6. Migrate cli/cmd test sites (agent_execute_bead_test.go's two
     fake runners, agent_run_config_test.go remnants).

  7. Migrate cli/internal/server (server.go RunOptions
     construction + workers_stop_propagation_test.go mock).

  8. THEN run this cleanup bead as the no-op final-deletion
     bead it was designed to be: delete RunOptions /
     CompareOptions / QuorumOptions structs from types.go,
     delete the legacy Run method on Runner, rename
     RunWithConfig → Run. At that point the AC's `go build`
     and `go test` will pass on the existing tree.

After step 7, the tree should grep-clean for the three struct
names except in cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/testdata/src/agent/
(self-contained stub package, leave alone) and in analyzer.go's
package doc comment + runOptionsTypeNames list (which can be
updated cosmetically in step 8 alongside the deletion).

================================================================
Verification
================================================================

  cd cli && go build ./...   # green at base rev (b199cf3c)
  rg 'type (RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions) ' cli/
    # 3 matches in cli/internal/agent/types.go, all current

No code changes were made in this worktree.
rationale: Bead ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

result_rev=b199cf3cb11f50cfe842c5ad7faa9ac9db183ef7
base_rev=b199cf3cb11f50cfe842c5ad7faa9ac9db183ef7

Bead: ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

NO CHANGES — bead preconditions not satisfied; scope exceeds single-window budget.

## What is done

- Read the bead, SD-024, TD-024, runtimelint analyzer, and current state of
  cli/internal/agent/types.go and runner.go.
- Surveyed actual caller surface in the worktree (HEAD = 562f293c).

## Why no commit was made

The bead description states explicit dependencies:

  "Depends on bead 16 (Stage 1 cleanup) AND beads 17-21 (every Stage 2
   caller migrated)."

The bead's own reopen note (2026-04-26) confirms those dependencies are
NOT satisfied:

  "type RunOptions still exists at cli/internal/agent/types.go:36;
   QuorumOptions at :81; CompareOptions at :171. runtimelint reports
   ~80 violations across the tree using these types. Closure was fake
   (already_satisfied or premature). The retirement was NOT actually
   performed — only ExecuteBeadLoopOptions (B16) and ExecuteBeadOptions
   (B28) shipped real type-deletion commits."

So B17-B21 (the per-caller Stage 2 migration beads) were never done.
Today's measured surface, from this worktree:

  rg "RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions" cli/  →  188 references
                                                        across 48 files
  rg "RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{" cli/
                                                     →  ~50 composite literals
                                                        (~13 production,
                                                         ~31 in tests,
                                                         ~5 in lint testdata,
                                                         rest in agent
                                                         package internals)

Production constructor sites:
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:61, 261, 414, 685
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531
  cli/internal/agent/grade.go:169
  cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:82
  cli/internal/exec/store.go:201
  cli/internal/server/server.go:1913, 3786

Internal-implementation surface (beyond the two files the bead names as
"in-scope"): every dispatch helper in the agent package threads
RunOptions through its signature —

  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go,
  compare_adapter.go, execute_bead.go, routing_metrics.go, runner.go,
  script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go

plus BuildArgs, RunAgent, runVirtualFn, runScriptFn,
runClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult, processResult, and
TestProviderConnectivity all take RunOptions or fields from it.
CompareOptions and QuorumOptions both EMBED RunOptions, so deleting
RunOptions also forces a redesign of compare_adapter.go's option flow.

## What is blocking

The bead is framed as a one-shot type-deletion ("delete the structs,
rename RunWithConfig → Run"), but the AC requires `go build` and
`go test` to pass tree-wide. To pass those gates the bead has to
also:

1. Replace every internal RunOptions parameter (agent package
   helpers) with an AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig pair, OR keep
   them on a non-RunOptions internal type.
2. Migrate all ~13 production caller sites to RunWithConfig
   (renamed Run), including building a config.ResolvedConfig at each
   site (CLI, server, exec store, grade, compare_adapter,
   execute_bead, execute_bead_review, service_run).
3. Migrate or delete the CompareOptions/QuorumOptions surface
   (compare_adapter.go is heavily built around it; this is essentially
   a rewrite of the comparison/quorum dispatcher, not a rename).
4. Migrate ~31 test composite literals across agent_test.go,
   claude_stream_test.go, prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   session_index_test.go, models_test.go, virtual_test.go,
   plus runtime tests.
5. Update or remove evidencelint analyzer entries for RunOptions
   and the runtimelint testdata (which deliberately uses the legacy
   types as fixtures).

This is the SD-024 §"Migrate one production caller per bead" / §"Migrate
test files in groups" work — the ~5 beads (B17–B21) the bead description
points at as completed dependencies. Doing all of that here in a single
execute-bead window violates the SD's own per-bead size budget
("<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time, under $10 of model
spend") by an order of magnitude. The realistic LoC delta is in the
1500–2500 range across 30+ files.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Reopen B17–B21 (or their successor beads) with concrete per-file scopes
before re-queuing this bead. Suggested split:

- B17: migrate cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go RunViaService caller +
       cli/internal/server/server.go (2 sites). Add a
       config.ResolvedConfig handoff at each site.
- B18: migrate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go +
       execute_bead_review.go + service_run.go (3 sites; agent-package
       internal callers).
- B19: migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go and
       cli/internal/agent/grade.go — biggest surface; redesigns
       CompareOptions/QuorumOptions on top of AgentRunRuntime
       (likely promotes Harnesses/Strategy/Threshold/ArmModels/
       ArmLabels/Sandbox/KeepSandbox/PostRun onto a new
       CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime).
- B20: migrate cli/internal/exec/store.go + types.go.
- B21: migrate the ~31 test composite literals; update
       evidencelint analyzer + testdata.

Once those land green, this bead (B22) becomes the trivially-correct
final cleanup — delete the three types, rename RunWithConfig → Run,
update the runtimelint testdata that intentionally uses the legacy
names, and confirm runtimelint reports zero violations on the live
tree.

Recommendation: do NOT escalate this bead to a stronger model yet.
The blocker is the missing prior beads, not model capability. A
stronger model attempting the full ~2000-LoC migration in one
worker window would produce churn that is hard to review and likely
breaks one of the three production dispatch sites in a non-obvious
way (the historical bug class SD-024 was created to prevent).
rationale: Bead: ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

       config.ResolvedConfig handoff at each site.
- B18: migrate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go +
       execute_bead_review.go + service_run.go (3 sites; agent-package
       internal callers).
- B19: migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go and
       cli/internal/agent/grade.go — biggest surface; redesigns
       CompareOptions/QuorumOptions on top of AgentRunRuntime
       (likely promotes Harnesses/Strategy/Threshold/ArmModels/
       ArmLabels/Sandbox/KeepSandbox/PostRun onto a new
       CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime).
- B20: migrate cli/internal/exec/store.go + types.go.
- B21: migrate the ~31 test composite literals; update
       evidencelint analyzer + testdata.

Once those land green, this bead (B22) becomes the trivially-correct
final cleanup — delete the three types, rename RunWithConfig → Run,
update the runtimelint testdata that intentionally uses the legacy
names, and confirm runtimelint reports zero violations on the live
tree.

Recommendation: do NOT escalate this bead to a stronger model yet.
The blocker is the missing prior beads, not model capability. A
stronger model attempting the full ~2000-LoC migration in one
worker window would produce churn that is hard to review and likely
breaks one of the three production dispatch sites in a non-obvious
way (the historical bug class SD-024 was created to prevent).

rationale: no_changes rationale — ddx-ade5ebb3 (Stage 2 retire RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions + legacy Run)

WHAT IS DONE
- Surveyed full call graph for the three doomed types and the legacy Run method.
- Confirmed bead notes: dependencies (beads 17–21) ARE all closed, but they migrated
  only the public CLI command paths (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`,
  `ddx agent quorum`) — not the internal agent-package plumbing nor the
  external server/exec callers.

WHAT IS BLOCKING
The bead's stated in-scope is two files (cli/internal/agent/types.go,
cli/internal/agent/runner.go), but the AC requires `cd cli && go build ./...`
and `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass after the three exported types and the
legacy Run method are removed. Reality of references that must move
together (ripgrep counts):

A. agent.RunOptions / CompareOptions / QuorumOptions — 88 references in
   non-testdata Go files outside the lint testdata trees. Of these:

   Internal to cli/internal/agent/ (cannot be deleted without parallel rewrite):
   - virtual.go (runVirtualFn signature + builder)
   - script.go (runScriptFn signature)
   - claude_stream.go (4 helpers: resolveClaudeProgressLogDir,
     runClaudeStreamingFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult)
   - agent_runner.go (RunAgent), agent_runner_service.go (runAgentViaService)
   - models.go (BenchmarkArmsToCompare → CompareOptions)
   - grade.go (r.Run(RunOptions{...}))
   - service_run.go (RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner — public exported entry points)
   - session_index.go (SessionIndexEntryFromResult — exported)
   - routing_metrics.go (recordRoutingOutcome)
   - compare_adapter.go (~25 references — RunFunc, RunCompareWith, RunCompareViaService, RunCompareWithAgent, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, runCompareArmWith, RunQuorumWith, RunQuorumViaService, RunQuorumWithAgent, RunBenchmarkWith, plus the existing RunCompareWithConfigViaService/RunQuorumWithConfig adapters that still build CompareOptions/QuorumOptions internally)
   - execute_bead.go (AgentRunner interface signature + dispatchAgentRun + the runOpts builder around line 647)
   - execute_bead_review.go (DefaultBeadReviewer.dispatchReviewRun + the AgentRunner field at line 425)
   - runner.go itself (resolveHarness, resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock, BuildArgs, recordRoutingOutcome, TestProviderConnectivity, plus the legacy Run body and the Run alias for AgentRunner satisfaction)

   External callers (cli/internal/server, cli/internal/exec, cli/cmd):
   - cli/internal/server/server.go:1913 + :3786 (handleAgentRun + workers handler — both build agent.RunOptions{} for RunViaService)
   - cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 (AgentRunner interface signature uses agent.RunOptions)
   - cli/internal/exec/store.go:201 (builds agent.RunOptions for s.AgentRunner.Run)
   - cli/cmd/exec.go:98 (serviceExecAgentRunner.Run wraps RunViaService)
   - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462 (RunViaService call building RunOptions)

   Test mocks that implement AgentRunner (must update their Run signature
   in lockstep with the interface change):
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_runtime_test.go (capturingAgentRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go (reviewRunnerStub)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go (countingRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go (envIsoRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_e2e_test.go (gateTestAgentRunner, rationaleTestRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go (artifactTestAgentRunner)
   - cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror_test.go (artifactTestAgentRunner usage)
   - cli/internal/exec/store_test.go (mockAgentRunner)
   - cli/cmd/exec_acceptance_test.go (mockAgentRunnerCmd)
   - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture — also captures `last agent.RunOptions`)
   - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go (run-closure for RunCompareWith on line 584)
   - Plus the BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, "") test fixtures across agent_test.go (~16 sites) — these use the struct literal form, not interface satisfaction, so they migrate trivially via field-by-field replacement, but they still count.

B. Legacy Runner.Run satisfies agent.AgentRunner interface today. Several
   tests pass *Runner directly into ExecuteBeadRuntime{AgentRunner: runner}
   (integration_helper_test.go:184, execute_bead_runtime_test.go:56,
   execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go:35/85/116,
   execute_bead_e2e_test.go:358/403). Removing the legacy Run AND renaming
   RunWithConfig → Run breaks this satisfaction unless either:
     (a) The agent.AgentRunner interface itself is migrated to take
         (ctx, ResolvedConfig, AgentRunRuntime) — cascades to every mock
         listed in (A) above, AND to cli/internal/exec/types.go's
         AgentRunner because cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:152 assigns one to
         the other (Go's structural interface satisfaction).
     (b) An adapter type is introduced (e.g., agent.RunnerAsAgentRunner)
         and every test wiring rewritten — ~10 test sites.

WHY THIS IS A NO_CHANGES, NOT A BAD COMMIT
Two prior attempts on this bead were "fake-closed" (per the bead notes) —
likely because they tried to satisfy the AC verifier via the loophole that
the field_removed regex `\bX\s+[\*\[\]\w.]+` does not match
`type X = Y` aliases or `(opts X)` parameter lists. A type-alias
relocation (defining the structs in cli/internal/agentopts/ and aliasing
them back into the agent package) WOULD satisfy the regex check and the
manual rg over types.go alone, while keeping every external caller
working. But that is the same loophole that the bead author explicitly
called out and reopened twice for. The bead notes say: "Worker must
actually delete the three type definitions and migrate every call site."
Doing that requires the multi-file refactor described above.

That refactor is well-defined but is at least 30 files, ~150 hunks of
edits, and depends on a coordinated public-API redesign of:
  - agent.RunViaService / RunViaServiceWith (signature change)
  - agent.AgentRunner interface (signature change, cascades to exec.AgentRunner)
  - cli/internal/exec.AgentRunner interface (signature change)
  - The Runner.Run / Runner.RunWithConfig swap with a new private
    runInternal(opts) for internal callers (grade.go, service_run.go,
    runner.go itself, execute_bead_review.go) so they don't build a fake
    ResolvedConfig just to dispatch.

That is genuinely epic-sized — bigger than the four Stage-2 dependency
beads put together — and committing it half-done in this single worktree
risks leaving the build red on main.

WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
Either:

(1) Re-decompose this bead into ≥5 smaller beads that can each land green:
    - 24a "agent: introduce private runInternal + new agent.AgentRunner signature with RunArgs adapter type"
    - 24b "exec: migrate AgentRunner interface to (ctx, rcfg, runtime) signature; update store + cmd/exec wrapper"
    - 24c "server: migrate handleAgentRun + workers handler to agent.RunViaServiceWithConfig"
    - 24d "agent: migrate internal helpers (virtual, script, claude_stream, agent_runner, models, grade, service_run, session_index, routing_metrics, compare_adapter) off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions struct names"
    - 24e (this bead) "agent: delete the three exported type definitions + delete legacy Runner.Run + rename RunWithConfig → Run"
    Each prerequisite bead has a clear gate-verifiable AC and the final
    bead becomes a 2-file mechanical removal as originally scoped.

(2) Or: explicitly authorize this bead to expand its scope to the full
    30-file refactor and accept that it will land as one large PR. The
    bead description's "In-scope" lines and the AgentRunner interface
    discussion both need updating before a worker tries again.

The structural blocker is that the bead is written as a 2-file cleanup
but the AC + dependencies (which were not actually completed) require an
epic-scale rewrite. A stronger model will not solve that mismatch — it
needs either re-decomposition or an explicit scope-expansion ack.
